tutorialscreen_description_staffrideatte:
Ride Attendants operate your park's Rides and Transport Stations.


Attractions without a Ride Attendant will remain Closed until an Attendant arrives.


Tip! Keep an eye on your Staff Schedule to ensure your Rides are always staffed to avoid any interruptions!
tutorialscreen_description_staffschedule:
The Staff Schedule is made up of hourly slots, called Work Blocks. The Schedule sets a Work Block's task by default. You can also set a specific type of work task, Location, Zone, or Staff Building, through the Advanced Settings.


Break Blocks last for an hour, and can be set at any time except the beginning or end of a workday. Make sure to have enough capacity in a nearby Staff Building to allow Staff to take their scheduled Breaks. 


Empty Blocks represent hour slots where your Staff are not working. Staff are not paid for these Blocks.

The Staff Schedule allows you to manually assign work to your Staff, and set a specific Work Zone for them.


By default, every hour of a Staff member's workday is set to a relevant task, with one slot for a scheduled Break. The Schedule repeats daily, so any changes you make in the Staff Schedule will carry over into the next day.


If you manually place a Staff member in a different Zone outside of their Schedule, the Staff Schedule will show a Block Override for that hour. After this, Staff will resume their usual Schedule.
tutorialscreen_description_staffvendors:
Vendors operate your park's Shops and Restaurants.


Facilities without a Vendor will remain Closed until a Vendor arrives.


Tip! Keep an eye on your Staff Schedule to ensure your Shops are always staffed to avoid any interruptions!
tutorialscreen_description_stationsandtr:
Place more Station pieces to increase the size of the Coaster Station.


Longer Stations allow for more Cars in your Coaster Train.


You can change which side of the Station the Entrance and Exit gates are placed in the Station settings.


You can set the speed at which Coaster Trains Enter and Exit the station in the Station Utility Settings.


Tip! Longer stations and trains mean more guests can ride your coaster at once… but will also take up more space in your park.
tutorialscreen_description_thefirstdrop:
The first drop is very important, as this is where your coaster will gain the momentum it needs to start making its way around your track.


Make sure your track exits the first drop smoothly, as this drop can create a lot of longitudinal g-force! 


Tip! Be wary of adding sharp turns near the end of the first drop. Depending on the speed, this could cause a lot of g-force and raise guest Fear and Nausea rapidly!
tutorialscreen_description_timepark:
As you play, time will naturally pass in your park.

A park day is from 8am to 8pm, which are also the hours you plan out in a Staff Schedule. Your park never closes, but the clock does reset every day.

Time has an impact on weather and on your guests, who come and go at various times of the day.
tutorialscreen_description_toilets:
Your guests' Toilet need will decrease over time or when they eat or drink.

Guests will need to use a toilet when their Toilet need has fallen low enough.

With use, a toilet will get dirtier and dirtier until it reaches a state where guests will refuse to use it.

Janitors can clean up dirty toilets so that guests will be willing to use them again.
tutorialscreen_description_turningandban:
Experiencing too many turns at high speeds on your track can cause high amounts of lateral g-force, which is unpleasant for your guests.  


To prevent this, you can twist the track into the turn. This is called Banking and allows you to transform a lateral g-force into a less uncomfortable vertical g-force.


Banking inwards to the turn causes the g-force to push the riders into their seats. Banking outwards causes a negative vertical g-force, which your guests won’t enjoy very much! 


To automatically soften sharp changes in track direction or banking angle, you can use the Smooth Tool. 


Tip! To lower the g-force in a turn, in addition to Banking, you can also make it wider!
tutorialscreen_description_undoredo:
Use Undo to roll back actions. You can Undo lots of things, like placing, moving, or deleting objects.

After Undoing an action, you can use Redo to do it again, assuming that you haven't done any other actions in the meantime.

But remember, any money gained or lost by an action is also affected if you Undo or Redo that action.
tutorialscreen_description_weatherseason:
In the bottom right of the screen you'll find a Weather Forecast. This helpful tool will tell you the current weather, what weather is coming up today, and what weather is coming up over the next 2 days. You can also see how these weather types affect your park and guests.


Guests will be more inclined to navigate towards different attractions based on the weather. A coaster in the pouring rain isn't very appealing!


Different Seasons and Biomes have different weather patterns, so some weather will be more or less likely depending on when and where your park is.
tutorialscreen_description_workshop:
You can find your creations and download the creations of other community members by visiting the Frontier Workshop. Click on Go to Creation on the Most Popular tab or any thumbnails on the browse menu to open the details.

Here you can click on Subscribe to download the blueprint, see the tags to search for similar creation, view the author profile to see their others creations but most important you can like (or dislike) to show your appreciation, put it on your Favourite for finding it later or report it.

The different tags and filters will help you find the best blueprints for your taste. And if you have it you can enter a blueprint code to access private blueprints shared to you.
tutorialscreen_image_basicgameplay_1: Build rides, pools, and scenery to increase your park rating and pull in more guests.
tutorialscreen_image_basicgameplay_2: Set prices for attractions and shops to earn money from guests.
tutorialscreen_image_basicgameplay_3: To make profit, you need to earn more income than the overall running costs of your park.
tutorialscreen_image_blueprints_2: Give your Blueprint a title and description to identify it to other players. Choose categories, a browser location, and add filters to help other players find appropriate creations quickly. Choose your blueprint thumbnail to make it stand out.
tutorialscreen_image_camerabasics_1: Explore your park by moving the camera forwards, backwards, and sideways.
tutorialscreen_image_camerabasics_2: Stay in one place and rotate your view to look around.
tutorialscreen_image_camerabasics_3: Zoom out to get a nice wide view of your park… Then Zoom right back in to see every detail again!
tutorialscreen_image_drawtool_1: The Draw Tool creates shapes one side at a time. It will complete the shape when you connect back to its starting point.
tutorialscreen_image_drawtool_2: Curved lines create organic shapes, perfect for swimming pools. Mix and match your curves with straight lines for great looking paths and plazas.
tutorialscreen_image_drawtool_3: Snap to existing corners to fill any gaps between paths, or to overwrite existing path and pool sections.
tutorialscreen_image_edittool_1: The Edit Tool can select points and lines from the edges of existing shapes. This allows you to make precise adjustments to aspects, such as path angle or pool depth.
tutorialscreen_image_edittool_2: To apply various changes at once, select multiple connected points and lines.
tutorialscreen_image_edittool_3: While you have an active Selection, you can open the Draw Tool to immediately edit the selected points and lines.
tutorialscreen_image_finances_1:
To grow your park you'll need money.

You can see how much you're earning in the Finances Screen.
tutorialscreen_image_finances_2: The Finances Screen allows you to see your income and all of your park's costs at various levels of detail.
tutorialscreen_image_finances_3:
If you find yourself low on money, the Loans screen can help.

You'll get an immediate cash injection, but you'll need to pay back more than you borrow.

Plus, there will be a Sales Cut that increases your costs until the loan is repaid.
tutorialscreen_image_foodanddrink_1: Guests will get hungry and thirsty while in the park. Go to the Group Tolerances tab in the guest panels to find out what foods your guests can't eat.
tutorialscreen_image_foodanddrink_2: Look at each shop to see what items are on the menu.
tutorialscreen_image_foodanddrink_3: Placing multiple different food and drink shops in the park will make sure that guests are able to find somewhere to eat when they're hungry or thirsty.
tutorialscreen_image_guestexperience_1: Guests will score their experience in the park based on the moods and thoughts they've had since they arrived. Their score will constantly change as they experience new things, so even the grumpiest guest can have their score turned around if they have enough fun.
tutorialscreen_image_guestexperience_2: All guest experiences are collated into a single guest experience value in the park management screen.
tutorialscreen_image_guestexperience_3: Guest experience feeds into your Park Reputation, which directly affects Park Rating. Good reviews from guests really matter!
tutorialscreen_image_guestmoods_1: Guests will show their feelings about your park by displaying a variety of moods. Keeping guests happy means catering to their needs and giving them lots of fun things to do.
tutorialscreen_image_guestmoods_2: Guests usually have thoughts about why they are in a specific mood, and this will give you a clue as to what is working (or not) in your park.
tutorialscreen_image_guestmoods_3: Guests who've enjoyed themselves and had more positive experiences than bad ones in your park will have a higher score in their Guest Experience panel.
tutorialscreen_image_guestneeds_1: A guest with all their needs met is a happy guest indeed!
tutorialscreen_image_guestpanic_1: Panic strikes some guests on a path.
tutorialscreen_image_guestpanic_2: Guests having a bit of a panic in the pool.
tutorialscreen_image_heatmaps_1: Heatmaps can help you identify successful areas of your park, or areas that you could improve in various ways.
tutorialscreen_image_heatmaps_2: Most heatmaps will highlight big issues for you in red. Anything which isn't an issue will be blue.
tutorialscreen_image_heatmaps_3: Finding the biggest problem areas in your park is the first step to improving them!
tutorialscreen_image_hudoverview_1: The top left menu contains some important options: Park Management, Scenario Objectives, Park Expansion and Notifications.
tutorialscreen_image_hudoverview_2:
In the top middle is the Heatmaps option. 

Use heatmaps to visualise useful information by location, such as a buildup of hungry guests.
tutorialscreen_image_hudoverview_3: The bottom bar shows your current Park Cash, Guest Count and Park Rating. You can also access Undo / Redo, Time Controls, Camera and Lighting Options as well as the Weather Forecast.
tutorialscreen_image_lazyriver_1: Lazy Rivers are constructed using a series of Lazy River Jets along a narrow channel. Find these in the Pool Extras menu.
tutorialscreen_image_lazyriver_2: The Line Builder tool has settings to quickly place Lazy River Jets at regular intervals along a pool section.
tutorialscreen_image_lazyriver_3: Lazy Rivers increase the prestige of a pool, attracting more guests to float their worries away on their inflatables.
tutorialscreen_image_linearpaths_1: All Rides must be accessible from the Park Entrance. This can be done by connecting paths.
tutorialscreen_image_linearpaths_2: Change the Line Style in the path preview to switch between straight and curved paths.
tutorialscreen_image_linearpaths_3: Raise path end points into the air to elevate paths and create stairs, bridges, and underpasses.
tutorialscreen_image_linearpaths_4: You can change Line Type, or choose when terrain follows the path that you are building.
tutorialscreen_image_linearpaths_5: Change Connection Type to choose how the new path joins and leaves other paths.
tutorialscreen_image_linearpaths_6: Change the Line Width setting to increase the path width.
tutorialscreen_image_linearpaths_7: Easily build repeating sections of path using settings like Fixed Length to consistently place down the same length of path. Do this with a curved path to make a snaking route!
tutorialscreen_image_linearpaths_8: Angle Snap allows you to place paths with consistent curves.
tutorialscreen_image_linearpaths_9: Snap Height is perfect for making consistent staircases.
tutorialscreen_image_maintenance_1: A Mechanic servicing a ride like a pro.
tutorialscreen_image_marketing_1: Advertising is an excellent way to bring more guests to your park!
tutorialscreen_image_marketing_2: Simply choose the demographic that your advertising will target, assign how much you want to spend over time, and wait for your advertising campaign to do its magic.
tutorialscreen_image_marketing_3: Some guests will have preferences depending on their demographic. Appealing to guest preferences will encourage them to spend more money at your park.
tutorialscreen_image_openingpools_1: Guests always need to do a couple of things before being able to take the plunge into a pool.
tutorialscreen_image_openingpools_2: They will need a Pool Pass which they can buy from Guest Services or the Park Entrance.
tutorialscreen_image_openingpools_3: Once guests have their Pool Pass, they need somewhere to change into their swimwear. Place a Changing Room down and they'll be pool ready in no time!
tutorialscreen_image_parkmanagement_1: Park Options: Customise your park's details, review your Park Security stats and Weather Management.
tutorialscreen_image_parkmanagement_2: Park Rating: Review your Park Rating, and see the requirements for the next star.
tutorialscreen_image_parkmanagement_3: Guests: See what guests think of your park, and the demographics you can target with advertising.
tutorialscreen_image_parkmanagement_4: Attractions and Shops: Review your rides, pools, shops, facilities and utilities to ensure they are in tip-top condition and performing well.
tutorialscreen_image_parkmanagement_5: Staff: See where your staff are assigned, and check their morale.
tutorialscreen_image_parkmanagement_6: Research: Spend your Research Points to discover new attractions and utilities for your park.
tutorialscreen_image_parkmanagement_7: Finances: Make sure your profits stay in the green. If you are in need of money quickly, you can take out a loan to pay for whatever you need.
tutorialscreen_image_parkrating_1:
Park Rating shows how much guests love your park! Increasing your Park Rating means more guests will come to your park.


Park Rating is decided by four key factors: Attraction Prestige, Scenery Rating, Unique Attractions, and Park Reputation.


The total Attraction Prestige of all rides in your park will contribute to your Park Rating. Check each ride's individual Prestige to see how it can be improved.


Scenery Rating shows how much guests are enjoying the look of your park. Place lots of varied scenery to improve your scenery score.
tutorialscreen_image_parkrating_1_old:
Park Rating shows how much guests love your park!


Increasing your Park Rating means more guests will come to your park.


Park Rating is decided by four key factors: Attraction Prestige, Scenery Rating, Unique Attractions, and Park Reputation.
tutorialscreen_image_parkrating_2:
Guests will also think your park is boring if all your rides are the same. Placing Unique Attractions is a sure-fire route to improving your Rating!


Lastly, your Park Reputation is determined by your Guest Experience. Check in on what your guests are thinking and feeling to see what needs changing in your park.


Tip! The lowest scoring factor will have the biggest impact on your Rating - check your score in Park Management regularly to see which score to improve!
tutorialscreen_image_parkrating_2_old:
The total Attraction Prestige of all rides in your park will contribute to your Park Rating. Check each ride's individual Prestige to see how it can be improved.


Scenery Rating shows how much guests are enjoying the look of your park. Place lots of varied scenery to improve your scenery score.
tutorialscreen_image_parkzones_1: You can draw Staff Zones with the Brush Tool, organising the park into smaller sections, each with their own colour and icon.
tutorialscreen_image_parkzones_2: Staff can be assigned to Staff Zones to improve efficiency. Staff members will focus on the Zone they're assigned to, instead of walking long distances all the way across your park.
tutorialscreen_image_parkzones_3: You can examine information per Staff Zone, including Rides, Scenery Rating and Finances.
tutorialscreen_image_pathextras_1: Benches allow guests to recover their Energy need, improving their park experience and letting them stay longer!
tutorialscreen_image_pathextras_2: Guests can sit down to eat at picnic benches when they have food or drink.
tutorialscreen_image_pathextras_3: Guests create litter after finishing food or drink. They'll throw litter into bins if they pass one, but they'll also drop the litter on the ground if they carry it for too long.
tutorialscreen_image_pathtypes_1: Standard Paths are the most common walkway in a park, as they support both guests and staff.
tutorialscreen_image_pathtypes_2:
Queue Paths are where guests will wait to get on a ride. Use these to connect Standard Paths to rides.


Don't forget to add Scenery to keep your guests entertained while they're waiting!
tutorialscreen_image_pathtypes_3: Staff Paths can only be accessed by staff. Use these to provide them with quick access to certain parts of your park, like Staff Facilities or Utilities.
tutorialscreen_image_poolextras_1: Pool Extras are scenery pieces that can add convenience and excitement to your pools! Make sure you add them to your pools to increase the Pool Prestige and draw in even more guests!
tutorialscreen_image_poolextras_2:
Sun Loungers, Diving Boards, and Pool Ladders are all Pool Extras.


Tip! Check your pool's InfoPanel to see how your Pool Extras affect your Pool Prestige!
tutorialscreen_image_poolprestige_1: Remember, with great pools comes great prestige... A pool with high prestige attracts lots of guests willing to pay extra to have a swim.
tutorialscreen_image_poolprestige_2: Scenery, Water Jets, Wave Machines, Pool Extras and Flumes all add to a pool's prestige.
tutorialscreen_image_power_1:
Rides and Facilities need to be connected to a Power Source to work.


Find Power Sources under Utilities, in the Facilities section of the Browser. Your Park Entrance can also provide some power when you are starting out.
tutorialscreen_image_power_2:
Power Sources, like Power Generators and Solar Panels, must be connected to Power Distributors to create a power grid.


Power Distributors take Power from a connected Power Source and distribute this Power to everything within its radius.
tutorialscreen_image_power_3:
Rides and Facilities that are not within a Power Distributor's radius will not be powered.


Tip! Make sure your Power Generators are maintained by a Mechanic so they can continue to produce maximum Power output!
tutorialscreen_image_pricing_1:
Pricing Modes set the prices that Guests will pay for things in your park.


There are a number of automatic Pricing Modes to help you get started.
tutorialscreen_image_pricing_2:
Custom - Lets you tweak the price to whatever you'd like.


Bargain - A cheaper price that all of your guests will be willing to pay.


Premium - A higher price that not all guests will tolerate.
tutorialscreen_image_pricing_3:
Dynamic - An advanced mode where you set a range and prices adjust within that range based on the current queue length.


Tip! Check the Finances tab of Park Management to see how successful your current prices are.
tutorialscreen_image_pricing_3_alternati:
Free - Park entry is free to all guests.

Admission Only - Guests must pay for both park admission and ride tickets.

Dynamic - An advanced mode where you set a range and prices adjust within that range based on the current queue length.
tutorialscreen_image_research_1:
Research is how you unlock new rides and facilities to place in your park.


Use Research Points to unlock Research. Assign a Mechanic to a Mechanic Workshop to generate Research Points.
tutorialscreen_image_research_2:
The Research tab is found in the Park Management screen, or in Chapter Select in Career Mode.


Unlocked Research is available in both Career and Sandbox modes.
tutorialscreen_image_research_3:
Some Research is locked behind Chapter progression. Both researching previous nodes and progressing through the campaign will give you access to new Research items.


Tip! Check back on the Research screen often, to see what new facilities you can offer your guests!
